text:
We propose Graph of Thoughts (GoT), a framework that advances the reasoning capabilities of large language models (LLMs) significantly compared to state-of-the-art approaches such as Chain-of-Thought (CoT) or Tree of Thoughts (ToT). Graph of Thoughts considers the LLM output as a graph in general form. Here, the nodes are the so-called thoughts and the edges represent the relationship between them.

Going beyond the linear or tree-like structure of previous models, GoT enables the model to generate non-trivial compositions of individual input ideas, distill the underlying insights from intricate reasoning, and performs several iterations of postprocessing and refinements via cyclic feedback. Experiments conducted demonstrate that GoT surpasses the existing models, achieving up to 62% higher sorting quality (comparing to 39% for ToT) and more than 31% faster computation speed. In addition, the modularity of GoT allows for easier integration of future thought transformations as well as for supporting diverse forms of future prompting techniques. Therefore, our model is in better alignment with the fundamentally non-linear and recurrent processing characteristics of the human brain.
headline: Fully Human Written
prediction: We believe that this document is fully human-written
